of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the timeToLive configurations</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, the Message is flagged to avoid generating its MessageID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable Message Timestamp</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, the Message is flagged to avoid generating its sent Timestamp</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delivery Delay</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only used by JMS 2.0. Sets the delivery delay to be applied to postpone the Message delivery</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delivery Delay Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the deliveryDelay configurations</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ack Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The Session ACK mode to use when consuming the message</p></td><td><p>IMMEDIATE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Maximum Wait</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum time to wait for a message to arrive before timeout</p></td><td><p>10000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Maximum Wait Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the maximumWaitTime configuration</p></td><td><p>MILLISECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content type of the message body to be consumed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the message body to be consumed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#IBM-MQ-Attributes">IBM MQ Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- IBM-MQ:ACK
- IBM-MQ:CONNECTIVITY
- IBM-MQ:CONSUMING
- IBM-MQ:DESTINATION_NOT_FOUND
- IBM-MQ:ILLEGAL_BODY
- IBM-MQ:PUBLISHING
- IBM-MQ:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- IBM-MQ:SECURITY
- IBM-MQ:TIMEOUT

### Ack

`<ibm-mq:ack>`

Allows the user to perform an ACK when the AckMode#MANUAL mode is elected while consuming the
Message. As per JMS Spec, performing an ACK over a single Message automatically works as an ACK for
all the Messages produced in the same JmsSession.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Ack Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The AckId of the Message to ACK.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Throws

- IBM-MQ:ACK

### Recover Session

`<ibm-mq:recover-session>`

Allows a user to perform a session recover when the AckMode#MANUAL mode is elected while consuming
the Message. As per JMS Spec, performing a session recover automatically redelivers all the consumed
messages that had not being acknowledged before this recover.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Ack Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The AckId of the Message Session to recover.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Throws

- IBM-MQ:SESSION_RECOVER

## Sources

### On New Message

`<ibm-mq:listener>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Destination</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the Destination from where the Message should be consumed.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#topic-consumer">Topic Consumer</a></p></li><li><p>queue-consumer</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The Type of the Consumer that should be used for the provided destination.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ack Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The Session ACK mode to use when consuming a message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Selector</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JMS selector to use for filtering incoming messages.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Number Of Consumers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of concurrent consumers to use to receive JMS Messages.</p></td><td><p><code>4</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Timeout Minutes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Inactivity timeout in minutes. If no message is received within this period, the listener triggers a reconnection.</p></td><td><p><code>0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transactional Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_BEGIN</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of beginning action that sources can take regarding transactions.</p></td><td><p><code>NONE</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transaction Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>LOCAL</p></li><li><p>XA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of transaction to create. Availability depends on the runtime version.</p></td><td><p><code>LOCAL</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the content type of the received message. If the message already has a content type, it is overridden.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the encoding of the received message. If the message already has one, it is overridden.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore JMS replyTo header</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>True if no automatic response is needed for messages with destination header</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The body of the Message</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>JMS Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The JMSType identifier header of the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Correlation ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The JMSCorrelationID header of the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to send the body content type as a property.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content type of the messageâs body.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not the body outboundEncoding should be sent as a Message property.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the messageâs body</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reply To</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#MQJmsDestination">MQ JMS Destination</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The destination where a reply to this Message should be sent</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>The custom user properties that should be set to this Message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>JMSX Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#MQJmsxProperties">MQ JMSX Properties</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The JMSX properties that should be set to this Message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Persistent Delivery</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not the delivery should be done with a persistent configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Priority</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The default JMSPriority value to use when sending the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time To Live</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the default time the message is in the broker before it expires and is discarded.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time To Live Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the timeToLive configurations,</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, the Message is flagged to avoid generating its MessageID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable Message Timestamp</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, the Message is flagged to avoid generating its sent Timestamp.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delivery Delay</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only used by JMS 2.0. Sets the delivery delay to be applied to postpone the Message delivery.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delivery Delay Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the deliveryDelay configurations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Correlation ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Options on whether to include an outbound correlation ID or not.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Reply Pattern</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CORRELATION_ID</p></li><li><p>MESSAGE_ID</p></li><li><p>NONE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates how requests and reply messages are correlated, which ensures that the incoming message is the proper reply to the outgoing message.</p><p>* The <code>CORRELATION_ID</code> pattern value is used with the <strong>Publish Consume</strong> operation. After the operation publishes a request message to the destination queue, it expects a message with the same correlation ID as the request message. It then consumes that correlated response message.</p><p>* The <code>MESSAGE_ID</code> pattern value is used with the <strong>Publish Consume</strong> operation. After the operation publishes a request message to the destination queue, it expects a message with a correlation ID of the same value as the message ID of the request message. It then consumes that correlated response message.</p></td><td><p>CORRELATION_ID</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#IBM-MQ-Attributes">IBM MQ Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

## Types

### TLS

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enabled Protocols</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enabled Cipher Suites</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of CipherSuites enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trust Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revocation Check</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location of the trust store which resolves relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insecure</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, no certificate validations are performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location of the key store which resolves relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file is used by default.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Only End Entities</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefer Crls</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Fallback</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Fail</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server cannot be reached or is busy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cert Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fails Deployment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance can be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### IBM MQ Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#JMS-Message-Properties">JMS Message Properties</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ack Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### JMS Message Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>All</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jms Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#jmsx-properties">JMSX Properties</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### JMSX Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Jmsx User ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx App ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Delivery Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Group ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Group Seq</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Producer TXID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Consumer TXID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Rcv Timestamp</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Redelivery Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering process-failed-message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Secure Hash</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Digest Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use.</p></td><td><p><code>SHA-256</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id Expression</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if useSecureHash is false.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is going to be stored.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### MQ JMS Destination

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Destination</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name that identifies the destination where a message should be sent.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Destination Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>QUEUE</p></li><li><p>TOPIC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of this destination.</p></td><td><p>QUEUE</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### MQ JMSX Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Jmsx User ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx App ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Delivery Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Group ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Group Seq</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Producer TXID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Consumer TXID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmsx Rcv Timestamp</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Topic Consumer

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Durable</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shared</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Local</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Binding

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Queue Manager</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The queue manager name.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Client

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host where the IBM MQ Broker is.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Queue Manager listener port.</p></td><td><p><code>1414</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue Manager</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The queue manager which is used when selecting a channel definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the channel to connect to.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Name List</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Hosts to which the client attempts to reconnect to after its connection is broken. The connection name list is a comma-separated list of host and IP port pairs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Reconnect Options</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RECONNECT</p></li><li><p>RECONNECT_Q_MGR</p></li><li><p>AS_DEF</p></li><li><p>DISABLED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Controls the reconnection behavior for the IBM MQ client. <code>RECONNECT</code> allows reconnection to any queue manager in the cluster, which is required for IBM MQ Uniform Cluster automatic application balancing. <code>RECONNECT_Q_MGR</code> restricts reconnection to the same queue manager. Applies when a Connection Name List or CCDT is configured.</p></td><td><p><code>RECONNECT</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Reconnect Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum time in seconds that the client spends attempting to reconnect. Applies when Connection Name List is configured with multiple hosts.</p></td><td><p><code>20</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Default Caching

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Session Cache Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the session cache.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cache Producers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether or not to cache the producer.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cache Consumers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether or not to cache the consumer.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# IBM MQ Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

IBM MQ Connector v1.8

Anypoint Connector for IBM MQ (IBM MQ Connector) enables an app to do messaging to a IBM MQ Broker
using the IBM MQ JMS implementation. The main features include:

- Pub/Sub pattern support on any given destination.
- Listen/Reply pattern support on any given destination.
- Publish-Consume pattern support on any given destination, with fixed or temporary reply Queue.
- Non-blocking publishing and consumption of messages.

Release Notes: [IBM MQ Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/connector-ibm-mq)  
Exchange: [IBM MQ Connector](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.connectors/mule-ibm-mq-connector/)

## Connection Mode

When creating the IBM configuration, choose is the
[IBM connection mode](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.0.x?topic=SSFKSJ_9.0.0/com.ibm.mq.dev.doc/q031720_.html)
to use.

As of IBM MQ 8, there are only two available connection modes, **Client** and **Binding**.

### Client Connection Mode

The client connection mode lets you connect to the IBM MQ Queue Manager running in the same system
that is being run the Mule Application or in other system. In both cases the IBM MQ Connector will
connect to the IBM MQ Queue Manager over TCP/IP.

For this connection mode are available the following configurations:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Required</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td><td></td><td><p>Host containing the IBM MQ Broker.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td><td><p>1414</p></td><td><p>Queue manager listener port.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue Manager</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td><td></td><td><p>The queue manager used when selecting a channel definition.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td><td></td><td><p>Name of the channel to connect to.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Name List</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td><td></td><td><p>Hosts to which the client attempts to reconnect after its connection is broken.<br>The connection name list is a comma separated list of host and IP port pairs.<br><strong>Example:</strong> <code>0.0.0.0(1414),somehost.domain.com(1415)</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

![Client Connection](_images/ibm-mq-client-connection-mode.png)

Figure 1. Client Connection Configuration on Studio

Client Connection Configuration on XML

```xml
<ibm-mq:config name="IBM_MQ_Config">
 <ibm-mq:connection >
   <ibm-mq:connection-mode >
	 	<ibm-mq:client host="0.0.0.0" port="1414" queueManager="QM1" channel="DEV.ADMIN.SVRCONN" />
		</ibm-mq:connection-mode>
	</ibm-mq:connection>
</ibm-mq:config>
```

### Binding Connection Mode

The binding connection mode connects automatically to the IBM MQ broker running in the same system.

The unique parameter that is optionally configurable is the Queue Manager to connect to. If the IBM
MQ broker has a default Queue Manager, this configuration is not required.

![Client Connection](_images/ibm-mq-binding-connection-mode.png)

Figure 2. Binding Connection Configuration on Studio

Binding Connection Configuration on XML

```xml
<ibm-mq:config name="IBM_MQ_Config">
  <ibm-mq:connection >
    <ibm-mq:connection-mode >
      <ibm-mq:binding queueManager="QM1" />
    </ibm-mq:connection-mode>
  </ibm-mq:connection>
</ibm-mq:config>
```

## Define Global Defaults

The IBM MQ `config` allows you to define multiple parameters to use as a default while consuming or
publishing messages. This way, you can define a global default behavior for all the operations
associated with the config and override each parameter only in the operations that require a custom
behavior.

For example, we can define a default `selector` behavior when consuming a Message in any of the
operations that are associated to this config, and also a default.

```xml
<ibm-mq:config name="IBM_MQ_Config">
  <ibm-mq:connection/>
  <ibm-mq:consumer-config selector="JMSType = `INVOICE`"/>
  <ibm-mq:producer-config timeToLive="60000"/>
</ibm-mq:config>
```

In this case, every consume or listener operation associated with this configuration uses the
`selector` by default, while every time a publish or publish-consume occurs, the outgoing message
has a TTL of 60 seconds.

All of these properties can be overridden at operation level when required. To publish a message
with a shorter TTL, use this example configuration that overrides the parameter:

```xml
<ibm-mq:publish config-ref="IBM_MQ_Config" timeToLive="10000"/>
```

## Select the JMS Spec

The main common parameter is the JMS specification used to select between all the supported
specifications, which are 2.0 and 1.1. This specification must be supported by the broker
implementation associated to this connection, otherwise the connection fails.

> [!WARNING] Features available only in JMS 2.0 wonât work with any other specification.

## Provide Credentials For Authentication

To establish an authenticated connection, you need to configure the username and password in the
connection element:

```xml
<ibm-mq:config name="IBM_MQ_Config">
	<ibm-mq:connection username="<username>" password="<password>" >
		<ibm-mq:connection-mode >
			<ibm-mq:client
			host="0.0.0.0"
			port="1414"
			queueManager="QM1"
			channel="DEV.ADMIN.SVRCONN" />
		</ibm-mq:connection-mode>
	</ibm-mq:connection>
</ibm-mq:config>
```

## Configure Connections Caching

To connect with a broker and execute the required operations, the JMS connector creates multiple
sessions, consumers, and producers that can be cached and reused to increase the performance of the
application. Because of this capability, the IBM MQ connector allows you to configure which caching
strategy to use when creating new connections, caching by default both consumers and producers, and
preserving as many instances as possible in memory at the same time.

Customizing the cache configuration can be done directly in the connection declaration:

```xml
<ibm-mq:config name="IBM_MQ_Config">
  <ibm-mq:connection>
    <ibm-mq:caching-strategy>
      <ibm-mq:default-caching sessionCacheSize="100" consumersCache="false" producersCache="true"/>
    </ibm-mq:caching-strategy>
  </ibm-mq:connection>
</ibm-mq:config>
```

## Identify the Connection Client

The client identifier associates a connection and its objects with a state maintained on behalf of
the client by a provider, and it is **mandatory** for identifying an unshared durable subscription.

```xml
<ibm-mq:config name="IBM_MQ_Config">
  <ibm-mq:connection clientId="${env.clientId}"/>
</ibm-mq:config>
```

> [!WARNING] By definition, the client state identified by a `clientId` can be "in use" by only one
> connection at a time.

## Set Up the Connection Required Libraries

**Important:** No matter what type of connection you are using, you always need to configure a
library containing the JMS client implementation, since the connector is not bound to any particular
implementation.

### IBM MQ External Libraries

To use the IBM MQ Connector, configure the external IBM MQ library, The IBM MQ Client library must
provide the `com.ibm.mq.jms.MQConnectionFactory` implementation.

> [!TIP] Use the IBM MQ Allclient library.

For example, you can use:

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.ibm.mq</groupId>
    <artifactId>com.ibm.mq.allclient</artifactId>
    <version>9.0.5.0</version>
</dependency>
```

## See Also

- [Publish Messages](ibm-mq-publish)
- [Listen For New Messages](ibm-mq-listener)
- [Listen For A Reply](ibm-mq-publish-consume)
- [Handle Message Acknowledgement](ibm-mq-ack)
- [Handle Transactions in IBM MQ](ibm-mq-transactions)

- [Connector Reference](ibm-mq-xml-ref)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
